findings.title: The contract accepts arbitrary ERC721 transfers and permanently blackholes untracked NFTs

best_agent_match.title: The contract accepts arbitrary ERC721 transfers and permanently blackholes untracked NFTs

findings.title: Referral fee math charges the discount percentage as the final fee

best_agent_match.title: Referral fee math undercharges by using the discount as the final fee percentage

rejected_candidates.title: Proxy Admin Can Hijack Implementation to Malicious Contract
rejected_candidates.reason: Standard privileged upgradeability behavior; the admin's ability to upgrade implementation is an explicit trust assumption, not a distinct code bug.

rejected_candidates.title: NFT Burned Before Token Transfer - Potential Loss of NFT
rejected_candidates.reason: If the downstream `safeTransferFrom` reverts, the entire transaction reverts and the prior burn/state changes are rolled back; this is not a permanent-loss path.

rejected_candidates.title: NFT Burned Before ERC20 Transfer in Partial Withdrawal
rejected_candidates.reason: The ERC20 transfer uses `safeTransfer`, so any failure reverts the whole transaction and undoes the preceding burn/state updates.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation Prevents Recovery of Stuck NFTs
rejected_candidates.reason: `IERC721.safeTransferFrom` does not fail silently; a failed transfer reverts and rolls back `withdrawn = true`, so the described stuck state is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent Unlock Time Enforcement in splitLock
rejected_candidates.reason: After a split, the original and new locks are independent positions; extending one without extending the other is expected behavior, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No Zero Address Validation for priceEstimator in setFeeParams
rejected_candidates.reason: `setFeeParams()` gates `_priceEstimator` with `onlyContract`, so `address(0)` cannot be set through this function.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation of referrer Address in _chargeFeesReferral
rejected_candidates.reason: The caller chooses the referrer for their own transaction, so an unpayable or bad referrer only causes self-inflicted failure rather than a protocol-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value in Fee Collection
rejected_candidates.reason: The transfer return value is checked, and both `setFeeParams()` and `setCompanyWallet()` forbid `companyWallet == address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Integer Overflow in getFeesInETH Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Arithmetic uses SafeMath in Solidity 0.6.2, so the claimed overflow path is not supported.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Event Emission in recoverAssets
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of an event is an observability issue, not a standalone reportable vulnerability beyond the underlying asset-seizure backdoor already captured.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation of tokenAddress in lockToken Function
rejected_candidates.reason: Supplying an invalid token address mainly causes the caller's own transaction to revert; this is user error rather than protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent Access Control for whitelistAdmins
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing owner-designated whitelist admins is an intentional governance choice, not a security flaw by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Array Length Mismatch in depositsByWithdrawalAddress
rejected_candidates.reason: A deposit id has a single current owner, so `recoverAssets()` cannot naturally duplicate an existing id in `newRecipient`'s array under normal state transitions.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Initialization Check in Contract Constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: The proxy constructor already checks `delegatecall` success with `require(success)`; only intentionally skipped init data leaves the proxy uninitialized, which is covered by F-001.
